title: pyrus malus var. pendula
intro: Pyrus malus var. pendula, also known as the Weeping Pear or Pendula Pear, is a deciduous tree or shrub that is a cultivar of the common pear tree. The bark of the Pyrus malus var. pendula tree has been used in traditional medicine for its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Pyrus malus var. pendula include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and provide antioxidant protection. The herbal preparations of Pyrus malus var. pendula include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
